key_insight: A classical denoiser can be paired with an analytic Gaussian quantum component at sampling time to exactly recover nuclear quantum statistics, with the same learned model transferring across quantum contexts.
engineering_relevance: This enables reusable generative models for materials simulation that avoid retraining when temperature, isotopic mass, dissipation, or path boundary conditions change.
method: Train a denoiser only on classical Boltzmann data.
Compose it with a closed-form Gaussian factor that encodes the full quantum path-integral context.
Sample the resulting model to obtain exact nuclear quantum distributions, including open-path displacement and momentum statistics.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports exact transfer in theory and numerical experiments across temperature, isotopic mass, dissipation strength, and boundary conditions, without retraining.
watch_item: The exact guarantee holds only when the training noise stays below the intrinsic quantum uncertainty of the target ensemble, so noise calibration is critical.
paper_title: Nuclear Quantum Effects as a Denoising Problem
